behaviors.description: Speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels for attendee attribution.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure is not just local parsing; it is whether the STT model preserves/assigns speakers correctly. REAL-INFRA: real API call is involved, but the key risk is model output quality, not transport. LLM-DEP: yes (speech model output can vary and mis-attribute speakers). MULTI-STEP: not required by this behavior alone. Rejection gate: Workflow rejected because behavior text only requires STT label preservation, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run real meeting audio through the real STT API and assert transcript segments retain correct speaker labels for known utterances; verify downstream summary input can map statements to the right attendees.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is specific parsing logic on transcript text; failure boundary is a local function/object transform. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP: no, if parser input is transcript text fixture. MULTI-STEP: no. Rejection gate: Integration rejected because behavior text does not require cross-module handoff; it says 'parsing extracts' a structured pair.
behaviors.test_description: Feed parser representative transcript lines with explicit assignments and assert exact structured output (assignee, task text), including edge punctuation and multiple assignees.

behaviors.description: Live summary contains at least one action item for every attendee assigned a task.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: stubs can force perfect action-item coverage and hide real misses. REAL-INFRA: external call exists, but core risk is summary quality/completeness. LLM-DEP: yes, correctness depends on model deciding to include each assignee’s item. MULTI-STEP: could be single summarization step from prepared transcript. Rejection gate: Workflow rejected because behavior text checks one output property of live summary, not a required chain of multiple model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Use real transcripts with known per-attendee assignments, call the real summarizer, and assert each assigned attendee appears with at least one action item in structured output.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into `decisions` schema, not generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is routing/mapping logic once decisions are already identified; local transformation boundary. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP: no for this specific wording ('routes identified decisions'). MULTI-STEP: no. Rejection gate: Integration rejected because behavior text disqualifies upstream identification concerns and focuses on local schema routing.
behaviors.test_description: Given an input object containing pre-identified decision items, assert mapper outputs them only under `decisions` and not narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key transcript decisions appear in live summary `decisions` section, not buried in narrative.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: mocked LLM output can always place decisions correctly and hide real extraction/placement failures. REAL-INFRA: real call present, but deciding and placing key decisions is model-content dependent. LLM-DEP: yes. MULTI-STEP: not necessarily; can be validated on single summarization pass. Rejection gate: Workflow rejected because behavior text targets final section placement quality, not explicit multi-stage agent pipeline correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Run real transcript inputs with known key decisions through the real summarization call and assert those decisions are present in `decisions` with acceptable recall, not only in free-form narrative.

behaviors.description: For audio >60 minutes, service splits into segments, processes sequentially, and merges into one coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failures often emerge across stage handoffs (segmenting -> per-segment transcription/summarization -> merge). REAL-INFRA: likely real API/runtime constraints matter, but higher boundary is multi-step agentic flow. LLM-DEP: yes (segment summaries and merged coherence depend on model outputs). MULTI-STEP: explicitly yes; sequential multi-segment processing and merge. Rejection gate: Agentic rejected because behavior text explicitly requires chained processing/merge across multiple steps.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end on a real >60-minute recording, verify segmentation order, per-segment processing completion, and merged final summary coherence plus schema integrity.

behaviors.description: Full pipeline (upload, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes automatically for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: in-process mocks can hide orchestration and handoff failures across upload/transcription/summarization/delivery. REAL-INFRA: includes real I/O and external APIs. LLM-DEP: yes, pipeline contains model-driven stages. MULTI-STEP: explicitly full multi-stage agent pipeline. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior text requires full staged pipeline with model-dependent steps, which is beyond single infrastructure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Run a production-like end-to-end job with a real meeting file and real external services; assert successful completion, valid structured output fields, and no manual retries/intervention.
